8873C49623C34D03B8454A36853ABC4F: An image file can be assigned to a user by dragging and dropping the file directly onto the image upload area (if using a PC), or by clicking the upload area and selecting the image that you want to upload. The image's dimensions will be scaled down to 480 x 640px (3:4 aspect ratio) if they exceed this size, so it is recommended that uploaded images meet these conditions for optimal quality when uploaded. The image can be cropped using the Edit button, or deleted with the Remove button.
889B4903FC8A4EFCFFBED8613FBFD909: option above is enabled. This option allows additional inputs to be monitored during the Night (Instant) arm mode.
89E3A283E34E3CCB5AE107192AA6DAC2: The areas that you are able to control will be based on your user permissions which applies any time or control restrictions. Your web page profile will also determine if you are restricted to only view or view and control the areas.
8DA633CCF4FB04F9D5A90DA169A03F44: Inception allows multiple security areas to be defined, letting individual areas be armed and disarmed to provide more flexibility on site design and use. Certain sections of the site can be secured, such as server rooms and other critical areas, while others are freely accessible.
8DFF326CB154A03382157B9DB1B91A21: Allows users to be notified when an input in an area has been isolated. This lets a user know when a section of the system is potentially not being monitored when the area was armed. The areas that may be notified on are determined by the
8EA3D75A79AA464B9CF267CEDFBC4CC6: Readers can be configured to arm one or more areas when a user presents their card at the reader three times in a row in short succession. A user can only arm areas that they have permission to arm and the arm may fail if the area contains unsealed inputs. If this occurs, the user should use a more advanced interface to try and arm, such as the LCD Terminal or the web interface.
8ED184FF7A3EBDA48E07FF79DC7903B6: - For use on areas with multi-mode arming enabled, ignored otherwise. Full Area arms the whole area. Perimeter Mode arms the perimeter, with entry delay when someone opens a door. Night Mode also arms the perimeter, with instant alarm if any perimeter input activates.
8F031C133B500B0E1A29CD8C9B506373: To pass a Soak Test, an input must not generate a reportable alarm for the duration of the test. If no alarm is generated, the input will automatically return to normal operation at the end of the test. Otherwise, if the test fails, the input will remain in the failed soak test state until someone de-isolates the input.
904DF3492D017CF9E727517689F14445: Enables anti-passback logic on this door. Anti-passback adds additional validation of user access requests that check whether the user is already in the area they are trying to access. This uses the last known location of the user and is intended to prevent tailgating, moving throughout a building without following access control proceedures or passing a card back to allow someone else access to the area. Note that the user still requires permission to access the door.
90D470E53447DFEEDE27D8996725F648: - this will arm every area in the system that the user has permission to arm. The arm request will fail if the user does not have permission to arm any areas, or one of the areas they can control contains unsealed inputs.
90E6EA19833F47C92FC4D58646E78B88: If an arm request fails, the user can attempt to find which input was unsealed and fix it, or try to arm via an LCD Terminal or the web interface which will provide additional information for why the arm failed.
91CF67ACD8F6C7D17D5CA32AB6C165D4: If enabled, inputs on the exit path will auto-isolate after the exit delay ends. If the input returns to a secure state or the area is disarmed, the isolated input will automatically de-isolate. Inputs on the exit path are not included in the initial pre-arm input test when arming the area, this is commonly used to avoid faulty inputs or inputs that are delayed in entering a secure state from causing alarms to sound. The inputs that were late to secure would also automatically de-isolate once secure.
9235E9379D85E2751347CAC02186D471: Permissions determine what areas, doors and outputs a user can access or control on the system. The permissions also determine what items the user can control via the web interface. Permissions are created using a
925964A23FD75028967E29BFC90ED44D: The security PIN is a unique code that allows a user to control areas and outputs from an LCD terminal, or access a door from a Wiegand keypad reader.
93A9C86404FCD4F1E32BA4A335903C29: A custom input is an input that has no physical location or mechanism and exists only within the Inception Controller's logic. The custom input and the state that it represents can be used in the system as a normal input, being added to areas and assigned an input type, triggering reportable alarm events and activating area sirens. With no physical hardware, the state of the input is instead controlled by automated actions. These actions could be triggered by any state changes within the system, or by a 3rd party system via the 3rd Party Automation Interface, allowing reportable and actionable alarm events to be generated from other systems.
93DBF444003EAA5F1835AB42E07FF52F: - Certain Inovonics detectors are capable of reporting multiple alarms, this option is used to access the secondary alarm of the detector. This would be used, for example, with dual-input detectors that allow a separate device to be wired into the transmitter.
9412C70B603C3853781CF0C24625723B: Calendar exceptions are used when the same time change for a day should be repeated on multiple days throughout the year. The calendar can be updated, which could affect multiple Time Periods in a consistent way. A common use case example for calendar exceptions are extended trading hour days, where trading hours change consistently on many days throughout the year. The calendar in that case could apply to multiple time periods, like working hours, automatic door times and automatic arm times, each reacting to the same calendar. A user can then update the calendar throughout the year and the system reacts correctly.
949E3F08069B2948AD07747621D5E542: Commonly, the primary input for the area or the exit door reed switch or tongue sense is used, or a button on the outside of the building to ensure that the user has left the area before cutting exit delay short. The input(s) specified do not need to be inputs that are monitored by the area, any inputs in the system can be used.
957A4BD040A40C48F340145579C3A85C: button as a logged off access button. This also allows a user's location to be updated when used for PIN access with an area on the other side of the door.
95E7790CAAAE40C19A72341DC7001E0C: This page is used to create, edit and control areas.
9702C698F345A336EB6702A5888EAA2B: Note: If a user has overridden the state of a door to be locked or unlocked from the web interface, the override will take priority. However, when the followed area changes it's arm state, the override (unless overridden to Lockout) is automatically cleared and the door defaults to normal behaviour.
972D63D48A737BCF8A110D0662C5D1FF: If users will be controlling areas or outputs from an LCD terminal, it is important to make sure the user PIN length is long enough that it is unlikely the user PIN's could be guessed. A longer PIN number combined with the
97C0D51624CD51D0516C6FB1EAC439A9: - If selected the input will function as a standard input for buttons or switches such as a duress point. This input will not require EOL resistors and it is just a normal two state switch or button input. Once configured, this input needs to be programmed into an area to monitor its state changes or used as a trigger for automation.
9834440D8FE88388F352A69AE7850E91: time, it is recommended to choose a delay that is longer than what the user would typically need. For example, selecting two hours if the cleaner normally takes an hour and a half. This prevents the user from needing to repeatedly disarm the area each time the delay time was reached, while still allowing them to stay longer than the time if required on rare occasions by disarming again.
984526BAC2D9684234615C64C1F9D441: Provides an additional period of time before an area automatically arms to allow users to exit or manually disarm the area. The auto-arming of an area can occur because of area inactivity, scheduling or defer area arming. The auto-arm warn time can be used to generate an audible warning tone on sirens, siren outputs, readers and keypads. This allows the user time to either leave the area, or disarm the area to cancel the arm.
986850B8FBEEF825B9E11AF85CE5213E: This page will dynamically display the state of areas on the Inception Controller. This page also allows you to arm and disarm areas remotely via web interface.
98AEA13358C09B1A33A78B2EAF9797FC: The web profile can also include a list of areas, doors and outputs that will be shown in the favourite items section of the users dashboard, located on the top of the page. The favourites section allows the user to view the state of and control any items added to without having to navigate to the state/control page.
99900D31D02CAD193805B852CD955F3D: As the areas, doors, inputs, outputs and time periods change state in the system, the trigger condition is continually tested and when it changes from true to false or false to true, the correct set of actions are executed.
99E0B957C714E95BE92E17DA059AADD0: The area will automatically arm when the selected time period becomes active (the current time is inside of the specified times). This feature is commonly used to automatically arm the area late at night to ensure that the area is armed over night in case a user forgets to arm it.
99F1404996AC1F0E585FFC3D631B797D: To prevent accidental alarms during Perimeter mode, any perimeter inputs that may be commonly opened (doors, certain windows etc) can be set as Primary inputs. This will cause entry delay to begin in Perimeter mode and give a chance for the area to be disarmed. In Instant mode though, they will still go straight into alarm.
9B21A25A3645497B80A1B5FB2AB86EA8: Consider what items in the system should be scheduled and how they might be grouped together. You may need to create multiple time periods to handle these cases, such as when door free access should be allowed, when areas should automatically arm in the evenings, any scheduled automation required and when user permissions should be allowed or limited.
9B53A84F1532674B194C3CD4862562EC: When the area is armed it will monitor the state of the inputs. When one of the inputs added to the area is triggered, depending on the process group the area may activate sirens and siren outputs, send alarm messages to a monitoring stations etc.
9BA9165C14E811E2312D9C89E679F7C2: page, as well as their ability to control storage units from other pages. Control of storage units allows a user to unlock a unit, letting the unit be opened and accessed without an alarm. It also allows setting a unit to Vacant, preventing alarms or being marked as unlocked too long, or automatically resecuring after a period of time.
9BDF9E9FD65A745A66F216C19F0868C2: Disarming an area to reinstate it (which restores any unconfirmed or confirmed alarms and stops any confirmation timers), will remove those alarms from the group. For areas that confirmed their unconfirmed alarm based on this other area's alarms, if there are no other alarms in the group, then those areas may restore their confirmed alarm, reverting back to just an unconfirmed alarm and waiting for a second activation.
9C23E106C08171A2F1EAB4F3289E9E33: - When the input is triggered, if the area is armed and no alarms are active, Entry Delay mode will begin. If alarms are active or the area is not disarmed before entry delay ends, then alarm siren tones are played, strobes are enabled and an intruder event is sent to the monitoring station. If disarmed, the alarm responses will not occur. If tampered with, the same response will occur, except with a tamper event being sent to the monitoring station instead. This tamper response would occur regardless of the area's arm state.
9DCCF13F482454816A79E217A929C3FA: option above, which can alert users that the area will arm soon. This allows the user time to either leave the area, or disarm the area to cancel the arm.
9E0C8F9EEAD07B01AF47CFA694739B2B: This page is used to configure alarm reporting.
9E21D12B9CD90E26E400CF875CA51046: inputs for Wireless Doors, the corresponding *Low Battery* or *Poll Failed* input event will be sent to the monitoring station, regardless of the area's arm state. However, no strobes or sirens will activate. This provides a way for the various Wireless Door states to be reported without interfering with the standard intruder reporting patterns. Note that inputs that are not
9F7FC261A4D6C72A7A1FFB718E135F52: option enabled. This will allow sirens and alarms to occur if someone accesses a secure storage unit, but if it was an accident, then the units tenant can unlock the unit and the alarm can be cancelled.
9F9870BB94DDB75AB2AAE92592CFBE65: If enabled, the system will automatically isolate unsealed inputs in this area during the pre-arm test, rather than failing the arm if armed via a reader or a remote, or prompting a user to seal or isolate them if arming from an LCD Terminal / web interface. These inputs will remain isolated until either the input returns to a secure state, or the area is disarmed.
9FA57629E3CAE14BB53722C5CA203DE0: option transitions from active to inactive. For example, if a store should always be disarmed by a certain time for the start of business, a message can be sent indicating that the system has not yet been disarmed and opened.
9FB4744093293CE67C235F1ED4D5627E: If enabled, the Internal Siren driver (S1INT) on the module will be monitored for tampers and will be included in the overall system health monitor. To detect siren tampers, the siren devices need to be wired with a normally closed tamper switch.
A0470C9232702FFB6D4FB97FC2175E63: Allows one or more outputs to be associated with this storage block. If any units in the block are unlocked,secured or in alarm state, the outputs in this list will behave according to output behavior. If output behavior of an output is lighting, this will turn on output when unit is unblocked. If output behavior of an output is Secure Indicator, this will turn on output when unit is secure. If output behavior of an output is Alarm Indicator, this will turn on output when unit is in Alarm state.
A070FA3FCB4CD0C3FDB31F22F503E371: . The arm warning phase occurs prior to an area automatically arming by the system, allowing users time to exit the area or cancel the arm. The auto-arming can occur because of area inactivity, scheduling, or defer area arming. This condition remains true until the warning time has elapsed and the area arms, or the arm warning phase is cancelled.
A1029AC0F8FFDEF65B4359FDE4958DFA: - The T4000 is an Inner Range device that connects to the Inception Controller via USB. It provides multiple redundant reporting paths using multiple 3G networks in addition to Ethernet internet reporting. With all paths polled frequently, this ensures reliable alarm monitoring.
A208BD51978F455B85523CA96590D93B: option transitions from active to inactive. For example, if a store should always be armed by a certain time, a message can be sent indicating that the system has been left disarmed or that something is out of the ordinary.
A2D32667760FB796FB4227B4633A73E5: option above is disabled. This allows different notifiers to be created that monitor specific events for specific areas.
A3558011CAB22D100DFAA53477E6BBCA: This trigger condition will be true if the selected area has been configured with multi-mode arming and has been armed into the Night Mode mode, turning false when no longer armed or armed in a different mode.
A41E0A2704C727B3CEB9EC671F4B983D: - The search field will filter the currently displayed review events based on any text entered. This text will be searched across the Message, Who, What and Where columns. The text does not have to appear in a particular order, with each word separated by a space being searched for in a row. The results will display all the Review events that contain every entered word in the search field between the fields. This is commonly used to find specific events such as a user called Mary controlling the Foyer area. If the text

A660ED588FCD30FE69884D951C6DA69D: - When the input is triggered with the area armed and no entry or exit delay active, alarm sirens are played, strobes are enabled and an intruder event is sent to the monitoring station. If disarmed, the alarm responses will not occur. If tampered with, the same response will occur, except with a tamper event being sent to the monitoring station instead. This tamper response would occur regardless of the area's arm state

A7EA889E62FD168598BB556F9EEB3260: The entry delay is the duration of time that the user has to disarm an area without generating alarms. The entry delay is started when a user enters an armed area and triggers an input that is assigned the
A7F5B75C397154FB802E4FF9CA0B4973: This step is used to configure the Inception Controller and LAN modules. This will include the programming of inputs, outputs, doors and readers based on the type of module, as well as configuring module behaviour and which module health components have been utilized (for example, cabinet tampers, siren tampers, smart powersupplies, external battery).
A9082FE9FA3D17DCEC0096DF7F78858E: If the T4000 is configured for IRFast alarm reporting, then enabling this option will cause Inception to report IRFast alarms in Integriti-compatible format, rather than the legacy Concept-compatible format (default). The Integriti IRFast format adds support for reporting additional input state types that are not supported by the Concept IRFast protocol, e.g. Low Battery and Poll Failed.
AA66A9D9981192FAB1AE9F863BCCD341: This option prevents all users from logging into the LCD Terminal to perform any other tasks, for example, make programming changes, arm areas or control outputs.
AC314E54A815319F77D7FB824ECC2A7C: If doors are configured with inside and outside areas, numerous features can be used. User locations can update with access requests, the door can follow the area state to prevent unlocking when the area is armed, readers linked to the door can provide audible and visual feedback for area state changes and the readers can also be used to arm the area.
AC5E1788B3652599A000087582291D92: This option allows a user in the area to cancel an upcoming area arm without having to walk to a terminal or reader to do so. For example: a cleaner on the premises after hours may activate a motion sensor input to quickly cancel an arm warning if required.

AE82043FDE130E503897B7315450B693: . When the user requests to disarm the area, the user is first checked to see if they have permission to disarm the area normally. If not, they are then checked to see if they have permission to temporarily disarm the area. This allows normal disarm permissions to take precedence. This occurs with any disarm request from the user, be it from a terminal, the web interface, card swipe or remote fob button press.
AF5D72D78A89A85E46A2DF5C8E82B9A5: - When the input is triggered, alarm siren tones are played, strobes are enabled and a medical event is sent to the monitoring station. The medical response will occur regardless of the area's arm state. If tampered with, the same response will occur, except with a tamper event being sent to the monitoring station instead.
